content.text:
And lastly help me to crack this and make a reaadymade word file out of it - cat > /home/claude/make_extras.js << 'JSEOF'
const {
  Document, Packer, Paragraph, TextRun, Table, TableRow, TableCell,
  AlignmentType, BorderStyle, WidthType, ShadingType, VerticalAlign,
  PageBreak, LevelFormat, HeadingLevel
} = require('docx');
const fs = require('fs');

const W = 9360;
const cb = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 1, color: "CCCCCC" };
const B  = { top: cb, bottom: cb, left: cb, right: cb };
const tb = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 6, color: "1F4E79" };
const TB = { top: tb, bottom: tb, left: tb, right: tb };
const nb = { style: BorderStyle.NONE, size: 0, color: "FFFFFF" };
const NB = { top: nb, bottom: nb, left: nb, right: nb };

const sp  = (b=60,a=60) => ({before:b, after:a});
const r   = (t, o={}) => new TextRun({ text:t, font:"Arial", size:21, ...o });
const cr  = (t)        => new TextRun({ text:t, font:"Courier New", size:19, color:"C00000" });
const pb  = ()         => new Paragraph({ children:[new PageBreak()] });
const gap = ()         => new Paragraph({ children:[r("")], spacing:sp(50,50) });

// ── paragraph helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function p(runs, align=AlignmentType.LEFT, spacing=sp()) {
  const ch = Array.isArray(runs) ? runs : [r(runs)];
  return new Paragraph({ children:ch, alignment:align, spacing });
}
function cp(text, indent=640) {
  return new Paragraph({ children:[cr(text)], spacing:sp(18,18), indent:{left:indent} });
}
function bp(text, lvl=0) {
  return new Paragraph({
    numbering:{reference:"bullets", level:lvl},
    children:[r(text, {size:20})], spacing:sp(30,20)
  });
}

// ── section header ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function secBar(title, sub) {
  return new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
      borders:TB, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
      shading:{fill:"1F4E79", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
      margins:{top:140, bottom:140, left:240, right:240},
      children:[
        p([r(title,{bold:true, size:30, color:"FFFFFF"})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,30)),
        p([r(sub,  {size:19,  color:"BDD7EE", italics:true})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,0)),
      ]
    })]})],
  });
}

// ── topic card ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function card(icon, title, fromWhat, lines, fill="EAF3FB", codeLines=[]) {
  const content = [];
  // badge row
  content.push(new Table({
    width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W-60],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
      borders:NB, width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA},
      shading:{fill:"2E75B6", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
      margins:{top:60, bottom:60, left:160, right:160},
      children:[p([
        r(icon+" ", {size:22, color:"FFFFFF"}),
        r(title,    {bold:true, size:22, color:"FFFFFF"}),
        r("   [From: "+fromWhat+"]", {size:18, color:"BDD7EE", italics:true}),
      ], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,0))]
    })]})],
  }));
  // explanation lines
  lines.forEach(l => {
    content.push(new Paragraph({ children:[r(l, {size:20})], spacing:sp(28,20), indent:{left:160} }));
  });
  // code block if any
  if (codeLines.length > 0) {
    content.push(new Table({
      width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W-60],
      rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"F5F5F5", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:200, right:200},
        children:codeLines.map(l=>new Paragraph({children:[cr(l)], spacing:sp(16,16)}))
      })]})],
    }));
  }
  return new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
      borders:TB, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
      shading:{fill, type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
      margins:{top:0, bottom:100, left:0, right:0},
      children:content
    })]})],
  });
}

// ── two-col QA block ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function qaBlock(q, aLines) {
  return new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[Math.floor(W*0.42), Math.floor(W*0.58)],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[
      new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:Math.floor(W*0.42), type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"FFF9E6", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:160, right:160},
        children:[
          p([r("Q: ", {bold:true, color:"C00000", size:20}), r(q, {size:20})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,0))
        ]
      }),
      new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:Math.floor(W*0.58), type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"F0F7EE", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:160, right:160},
        children: aLines.map(l => {
          if (l.startsWith("CODE|")) return new Paragraph({children:[cr(l.slice(5))], spacing:sp(16,16)});
          return p([r(l, {size:19})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(20,16));
        })
      }),
    ]})]
  });
}

// ── full-width code box ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function codeBox(title, lines) {
  return new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
    rows:[
      new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"2E75B6", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:50, bottom:50, left:160, right:160},
        children:[p([r(title, {bold:true, size:19, color:"FFFFFF"})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,0))]
      })]}),
      new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"F8F8F8", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:200, right:200},
        children:lines.map(l=>new Paragraph({children:[cr(l)], spacing:sp(15,15)}))
      })]})
    ]
  });
}

// ── h2/h3 helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function h2(text) {
  return new Paragraph({
    children:[r(text, {bold:true, size:25, color:"1F4E79"})],
    spacing:sp(200,80),
    border:{bottom:{style:BorderStyle.SINGLE, size:4, color:"2E75B6", space:4}}
  });
}
function h3(text) {
  return new Paragraph({
    children:[r(text, {bold:true, size:22, color:"375623"})],
    spacing:sp(160,60)
  });
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// CONTENT
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
const children = [

  // ── TITLE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
      borders:TB, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
      shading:{fill:"1F4E79", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
      margins:{top:160, bottom:160, left:300, right:300},
      children:[
        p([r("R PROGRAMMING — REMAINING IMPORTANT TOPICS",{bold:true,size:32,color:"FFFFFF"})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,30)),
        p([r("Everything left from PYQ • CA1 • CA4 • PCA2 • Home Assignment • CA3 Topics",{size:20,color:"BDD7EE",italics:true})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,20)),
        p([r("BSc Biotechnology / Bioinformatics / AI  |  Semester 6  |  MAKAUT",{size:19,color:"9DC3E6"})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,0)),
      ]
    })]})],
  }),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 1. FILE I/O ══════════════
  secBar("1.  File Input / Output in R", "From: End Semester PYQ Q6A/Q6B  +  CA4 Q10"),
  gap(),
  h2("1.1  Reading Data"),
  codeBox("Read CSV file", [
    "# Method 1: direct path",
    "df <- read.csv('data.csv')",
    "",
    "# Method 2: GUI file picker",
    "df <- read.csv(file.choose())",
    "",
    "# Method 3: from URL",
    "df <- read.csv(url('http://example.com/data.csv'))",
    "",
    "# Inspect after reading",
    "head(df)       # first 6 rows",
    "tail(df)       # last 6 rows",
    "str(df)        # structure (column types)",
    "nrow(df)       # number of rows",
    "ncol(df)       # number of columns",
    "summary(df)    # basic statistics",
  ]),
  gap(),
  codeBox("Read Excel file (readxl package)", [
    "library(readxl)",
    "df <- read_excel('enzyme_data.xlsx', sheet = 1)",
    "head(df)",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("1.2  Writing Data"),
  codeBox("Write CSV and Excel", [
    "# Write CSV",
    "write.csv(df, 'results.csv', row.names = FALSE)",
    "",
    "# Write Excel (openxlsx package)",
    "library(openxlsx)",
    "write.xlsx(df, 'enzyme_results.xlsx')",
    "",
    "# Write output to a text file using sink()",
    "sink('output.txt')",
    "cat('Results:\\n')",
    "print(df)",
    "cat('Average:', mean(df$Salary), '\\n')",
    "sink()   # IMPORTANT: always close sink()",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("1.3  Full Enzyme CSV Analysis Program (from PYQ Q6A)"),
  codeBox("Enzyme dataset analysis — max, filter, save", [
    "# Assume input.csv has: Enzyme_ID, Enzyme_Name, Category, Activity_Level",
    "df <- read.csv('input.csv')",
    "",
    "# (i) Maximum activity level",
    "max_activity <- max(df$Activity_Level)",
    "cat('Max Activity:', max_activity, '\\n')",
    "",
    "# (ii) Record of enzyme with highest activity",
    "top_enzyme <- df[df$Activity_Level == max_activity, ]",
    "print(top_enzyme)",
    "",
    "# (iii) Filter by category AND activity above threshold",
    "category_filter <- 'Oxidoreductase'",
    "threshold       <- 80",
    "filtered <- df[df$Category == category_filter &",
    "               df$Activity_Level > threshold, ]",
    "print(filtered)",
    "",
    "# (iv) Save all results to result.csv",
    "write.csv(filtered, 'result.csv', row.names = FALSE)",
    "cat('Saved to result.csv\\n')",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 2. STRING OPERATIONS ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("2.  String Operations in R", "From: PYQ Q7B  +  Home Assignment (DNA/Protein functions)"),
  gap(),
  h2("2.1  Key String Functions"),
  new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[2200, 3400, 3760],
    rows:[
      new TableRow({children:[

  gap(),
  h2("2.2  ATG Codon Finder (from PYQ Q7A iii)"),
  codeBox("Find all positions of a given codon in a DNA sequence", [
    "find_codon <- function(dna, codon = 'ATG') {",
    "  dna      <- toupper(dna)",
    "  codon    <- toupper(codon)",
    "  codon_len <- nchar(codon)",
    "  positions <- c()",
    "",
    "  for (i in 1:(nchar(dna) - codon_len + 1)) {",
    "    if (substr(dna, i, i + codon_len - 1) == codon) {",
    "      positions <- c(positions, i)",
    "    }",
    "  }",
    "",
    "  if (length(positions) == 0) {",
    "    cat(codon, 'not found in sequence\\n')",
    "  } else {",
    "    cat('Codon', codon, 'found at positions:', positions, '\\n')",
    "  }",
    "  return(positions)",
    "}",
    "",
    "find_codon('ATGATCATGATCGGCATG')  # Positions: 1 7 16",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 3. APPLY FAMILY ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("3.  apply() Family Functions", "From: PYQ Q7A  +  Home Assignment  +  End Semester PYQ Q1.vii"),
  gap(),
  h2("3.1  apply() — for matrices"),
  codeBox("apply() on matrix rows and columns", [
    "m <- matrix(c(5.2,8.5,7.1,3.1,6.2,5.8,7.8,4.1,9.2), nrow=3, byrow=TRUE)",
    "rownames(m) <- c('TP53','BRCA1','EGFR')",
    "colnames(m) <- c('Control','Treat1','Treat2')",
    "",
    "apply(m, 1, mean)   # MARGIN=1 -> row means (mean per gene)",
    "apply(m, 2, mean)   # MARGIN=2 -> col means (mean per condition)",
    "apply(m, 1, sum)    # row sums",
    "apply(m, 2, max)    # column maximums",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("3.2  lapply() and sapply() — for lists"),
  codeBox("lapply returns list; sapply returns simplified vector", [
    "expr_list <- list(TP53=c(5.2,8.5,7.1), BRCA1=c(3.1,6.2,5.8), EGFR=c(7.8,4.1,9.2))",
    "",
    "lapply(expr_list, mean)   # returns a LIST of means",
    "sapply(expr_list, mean)   # returns a NAMED VECTOR — simpler",
    "# TP53  BRCA1   EGFR",
    "# 6.93   5.03   7.03",
    "",
    "sapply(expr_list, length) # length of each element",
    "sapply(expr_list, max)    # max of each element",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("3.3  tapply() and aggregate() — for groups (from PYQ Q1.vii)"),
  codeBox("Group-wise statistics on data frames", [
    "df <- data.frame(",
    "  group = c('Control','Control','Treated','Treated','Treated'),",
    "  value = c(5.2, 6.1, 8.5, 9.2, 7.8))",
    "",
    "# tapply: mean of 'value' for each unique 'group'",
    "tapply(df$value, df$group, mean)",
    "# Control: 5.65   Treated: 8.50",
    "",
    "# aggregate: same result, data-frame output",
    "aggregate(value ~ group, df, mean)",
    "",
    "# Both A and C correct — important MCQ point!",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 4. NA HANDLING ══════════════
  secBar("4.  Handling Missing Values (NA)", "From: PYQ Q1.viii  +  CA4 MCQ"),
  gap(),
  codeBox("NA handling — commonly tested patterns", [
    "v <- c(5, 2, NA, 8, NA, 4)",
    "",
    "mean(v)                  # NA  (NA propagates by default)",
    "mean(v, na.rm = TRUE)    # 4.75 (NA removed before calc)",
    "sum(v,  na.rm = TRUE)    # 19",
    "var(v,  na.rm = FALSE)   # NA  (MCQ: na.rm=FALSE -> NA)",
    "var(v,  na.rm = TRUE)    # 5.58",
    "",
    "# Check for NA",
    "is.na(v)                 # FALSE FALSE TRUE FALSE TRUE FALSE",
    "",
    "# Replace NA with 0",
    "v[is.na(v)] <- 0",
    "",
    "# Correct way in ifelse (WRONG: x==NA; CORRECT: is.na(x))",
    "x <- NA",
    "ifelse(x == NA,  1, 0)   # NA  (WRONG)",
    "ifelse(is.na(x), 1, 0)   # 1   (CORRECT)",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 5. SORTING & SUBSET ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("5.  Sorting, Filtering & Subset of Data Frames", "From: End Semester PYQ (Lab Exp 7)  +  CA4 Q10"),
  gap(),
  h2("5.1  Sorting with order()"),
  codeBox("Sort data frame rows by column values", [
    "emp <- data.frame(",
    "  Name   = c('Alice','Bob','Carol','Dave','Eve'),",
    "  Dept   = c('IT','HR','IT','Finance','IT'),",
    "  Salary = c(60000, 45000, 72000, 55000, 68000))",
    "",
    "# Sort ascending by Salary",
    "emp[order(emp$Salary), ]",
    "",
    "# Sort descending by Salary",
    "emp[order(-emp$Salary), ]",
    "emp[order(emp$Salary, decreasing = TRUE), ]",
    "",
    "# Sort by Department first, then by Salary",
    "emp[order(emp$Dept, emp$Salary), ]",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("5.2  subset() function"),
  codeBox("subset() — cleaner filtering syntax", [
    "# Employees with Salary > 60000",
    "subset(emp, Salary > 60000)",
    "",
    "# IT department only",
    "subset(emp, Dept == 'IT')",
    "",
    "# IT department with Salary > 60000 — select only Name & Salary",
    "subset(emp, Dept == 'IT' & Salary > 60000, select = c(Name, Salary))",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("5.3  Full Employee Data Frame Program (from CA4 Q10)"),
  codeBox("Complete employee program with file output", [
    "# a) Create data frame",
    "emp <- data.frame(",
    "  EmployeeID = c(101,102,103,104),",
    "  Name       = c('Alice','Bob','Carol','Dave'),",
    "  Department = c('IT','HR','IT','Finance'),",
    "  Salary     = c(60000,45000,72000,55000),",
    "  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)",
    "",
    "# b) Add new employee + update salary",
    "new_row <- data.frame(EmployeeID=105, Name='Eve', Department='IT', Salary=68000)",
    "emp     <- rbind(emp, new_row)",
    "emp$Salary[emp$Name == 'Bob'] <- 50000   # update Bob's salary",
    "",
    "# c) Filter IT department",
    "it <- emp[emp$Department == 'IT', ]",
    "",
    "# d) Average salary in IT",
    "avg <- mean(it$Salary)",
    "",
    "# e) Write all results to output.txt",
    "sink('output.txt')",
    "cat('=== All Employees ===\\n');   print(emp)",
    "cat('\\n=== IT Department ===\\n'); print(it)",
    "cat('\\nAverage IT Salary: Rs.', avg, '\\n')",
    "sink()   # close the connection",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 7. DPLYR & TIDYVERSE ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("7.  dplyr & tidyverse Packages", "From: CA3 Presentation Topics  +  End Semester PYQ Q4A"),
  gap(),
  h2("7.1  Core dplyr Verbs"),
  codeBox("dplyr — the grammar of data manipulation", [
    "library(dplyr)",
    "",
    "gene_df <- data.frame(",
    "  gene  = c('TP53','BRCA1','EGFR','MYC','PTEN'),",
    "  logFC = c(2.1, -1.5, 0.8, 3.2, -0.3),",
    "  pval  = c(0.001,0.005,0.08,0.0001,0.5))",
    "",
    "# filter() — keep rows matching condition",
    "gene_df %>% filter(logFC > 1)",
    "",
    "# arrange() — sort rows",
    "gene_df %>% arrange(desc(logFC))",
    "",
    "# select() — pick columns",
    "gene_df %>% select(gene, logFC)",
    "",
    "# mutate() — add/modify columns",
    "gene_df %>% mutate(neg_log_p = -log10(pval))",
    "",
    "# Chaining multiple steps with pipe %>%",
    "gene_df %>%",
    "  filter(logFC > 1) %>%",
    "  arrange(desc(logFC)) %>%",
    "  select(gene, logFC, pval)",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("7.2  tidyr — reshape data"),
  codeBox("pivot_longer and pivot_wider", [
    "library(tidyr)",
    "",
    "# Wide format (one row per gene)",
    "wide <- data.frame(",
    "  gene    = c('TP53','BRCA1'),",
    "  control = c(5.2, 3.1),",
    "  treated = c(8.5, 6.2))",
    "",
    "# Convert to LONG format (tidy format)",
    "long <- pivot_longer(wide,",
    "                     cols     = c(control, treated),",
    "                     names_to = 'condition',",
    "                     values_to= 'expression')",
    "print(long)",
    "",
    "# Convert back to WIDE format",
    "wide2 <- pivot_wider(long, names_from='condition', values_from='expression')",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("7.3  ggplot2 — Grammar of Graphics"),
  codeBox("ggplot2 layer-by-layer plot building", [
    "library(ggplot2)",
    "",
    "# Scatter plot — iris dataset",
    "ggplot(iris, aes(x=Sepal.Length, y=Petal.Length, color=Species)) +",
    "  geom_point(size=2.5) +",
    "  labs(title='Iris Dataset', x='Sepal Length', y='Petal Length') +",
    "  theme_minimal()",
    "",

    "df <- data.frame(country=c('Brazil','Canada','Mexico'), value=c(45,70,30))",

    "  geom_bar(stat='identity') +",

    "",
    "# Histogram",

args.description: Add a Bash security script and a professionally designed README to the IronClad template repository.
args.command:
# 1. Create a Bash version of the security script for Mac/Linux
$bashScriptPath = "C:\Users\skani\.gemini\tmp\system32\IronClad-AI-Template\secure_memory.sh"
$bashScriptContent = @"
#!/bin/bash
# secure_memory.sh - Iron-Clad Protocol for Mac/Linux
# Usage: ./secure_memory.sh lock   (to hide)
# Usage: ./secure_memory.sh unlock (to show)

ACTION=`$1
TARGET_DIR="`$(pwd)"

if [ "`$ACTION" == "lock" ]; then
    echo "Locking and hiding environment..."
    # On Unix, renaming to start with a dot hides it, 
    # but since this script is likely INSIDE the dir, we use chflags (Mac) or standard hidden naming
    if [[ "`$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
        chflags hidden .
    fi
    # Restrict permissions
    chmod 700 .
    echo "Protocol Active."
elif [ "`$ACTION" == "unlock" ]; then
    echo "Unlocking environment..."
    if [[ "`$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
        chflags nohidden .
    fi
    chmod 755 .
    echo "Protocol Standby."
else
    echo "Usage: ./secure_memory.sh {lock|unlock}"
fi
"@
Set-Content -Path $bashScriptPath -Value $bashScriptContent

# 2. Rewrite the README.md with high-quality instructions and design
$readmePath = "C:\Users\skani\.gemini\tmp\system32\IronClad-AI-Template\README.md"
$readmeContent = @"
# 🛡️ IronClad AI: The Secure Agent Infrastructure

> **Build the future of Biology without leaking your Intellectual Property.**

IronClad AI is a security-first framework designed for researchers, founders, and developers using local AI agents (like Gemini CLI). It provides a hardened environment to manage your career trajectory and proprietary research secrets without risking data exposure to the cloud.

---

## 🚀 The Methodology: "Surgical Strike"

This infrastructure separates your data into three distinct security tiers:
1.  **Context (Public/Shared):** High-level goals stored in ``GEMINI.md``.
2.  **Intellectual Property (Air-Gapped):** Sensitive scaffolds and algorithms in ``SECRET_IP.md``.
3.  **Local Shield:** Encryption and folder-hiding scripts to protect from unauthorized local access.

---

## 🛠️ Installation & Setup

### 1. Clone the Shell
```bash
git clone <your-repo-url>
cd IronClad-AI-Template
```

### 2. Personalize your Context
Rename ``GEMINI_TEMPLATE.md`` to ``GEMINI.md`` and fill in your background. 
**Note:** ``GEMINI.md`` and ``SECRET_IP.md`` are automatically ignored by Git—they will NEVER be pushed to the public web.

---

## 🔒 Security CLI Instructions

### For Windows Users (PowerShell)
To hide and encrypt your environment:
```powershell
.\SecureMemory.ps1 -Action Lock
```
To reveal your environment for editing:
```powershell
.\SecureMemory.ps1 -Action Unlock
```

### For Mac/Linux Users (Bash)
To hide and restrict permissions:
```bash
chmod +x secure_memory.sh
./secure_memory.sh lock
```
To restore visibility:
```bash
./secure_memory.sh unlock
```

---

## ⚠️ The Iron-Clad Warning
The ``.gitignore`` in this repo is your primary shield. **Do not modify it** unless you fully understand how to air-gap local data. Always verify that your ``SECRET_IP.md`` remains untracked before performing a git push.

---
*Architected by a Computational Oncologist & AI-Bio Founder.*
"@
Set-Content -Path $readmePath -Value $readmeContent

Write-Host "Repo refined with Bash script and High-Quality README."

Rename `GEMINI_TEMPLATE.md` to `GEMINI.md` and fill in your background. 
**Note:** `GEMINI.md` and `SECRET_IP.md` are automatically ignored by Git—they will NEVER be pushed to the public web.

---

To hide and encrypt your environment:

```powershell
.\SecureMemory.ps1 -Action Lock
```
To reveal your environment for editing:

### For Mac/Linux Users (Bash)
To hide and restrict permissions:

```bash
chmod +x secure_memory.sh
./secure_memory.sh lock
```
To restore visibility:

```bash
./secure_memory.sh unlock
```

---

## ⚠️ The Iron-Clad Warning
The `.gitignore` in this repo is your primary shield. **Do not modify it** unless you fully understand how to air-gap local data. Always verify that your `SECRET_IP.md` remains untracked before performing a git push.

# IronClad AI Agent Template
## Your Personalized, Secure AI Agent Infrastructure

This repository provides the infrastructure to build a "Surgical Strike" career and project management system using the Gemini CLI (or other local-first agents). 

### The Protocol
1.  **Context-First:** Use `GEMINI.md` to store your career goals and academic background.
2.  **Air-Gapped Secrets:** Use `SECRET_IP.md` for proprietary code and sensitive data (Excluded via .gitignore).
3.  **Local Encryption:** Use the included scripts to hide and encrypt your data locally.

### Setup
1. Clone this repo.
2. Create your own `GEMINI.md` using the template.
3. Run `SecureMemory.ps1` (Windows) to lock your environment.

---
*Created by a Computational Oncologist & AI-Bio Founder.*

# 🛡️ IronClad AI: The Secure Agent Infrastructure

> **Build the future of Biology without leaking your Intellectual Property.**

IronClad AI is a security-first framework designed for researchers, founders, and developers using local AI agents (like Gemini CLI). It provides a hardened environment to manage your career trajectory and proprietary research secrets without risking data exposure to the cloud.

---

## 🚀 The Methodology: "Surgical Strike"

This infrastructure separates your data into three distinct security tiers:
1.  **Context (Public/Shared):** High-level goals stored in `GEMINI.md`.
2.  **Intellectual Property (Air-Gapped):** Sensitive scaffolds and algorithms in `SECRET_IP.md`.
3.  **Local Shield:** Encryption and folder-hiding scripts to protect from unauthorized local access.

---

## 🛠️ Installation & Setup

### 1. Clone the Shell
```bash
git clone <your-repo-url>
cd IronClad-AI-Template
```

### 2. Personalize your Context
Rename `GEMINI_TEMPLATE.md` to `GEMINI.md` and fill in your background. 
**Note:** `GEMINI.md` and `SECRET_IP.md` are automatically ignored by Git—they will NEVER be pushed to the public web.

---

## 🔒 Security CLI Instructions

### For Windows Users (PowerShell)
To hide and encrypt your environment:
```powershell
.\SecureMemory.ps1 -Action Lock
```
To reveal your environment for editing:
```powershell
.\SecureMemory.ps1 -Action Unlock
```

### For Mac/Linux Users (Bash)
To hide and restrict permissions:
```bash
chmod +x secure_memory.sh
./secure_memory.sh lock
```
To restore visibility:
```bash
./secure_memory.sh unlock
```

---

## ⚠️ The Iron-Clad Warning
The `.gitignore` in this repo is your primary shield. **Do not modify it** unless you fully understand how to air-gap local data. Always verify that your `SECRET_IP.md` remains untracked before performing a git push.

---

Successfully created and wrote to new file: C:\Users\skani\.gemini\tmp\system32\IronClad-AI-Template\secure_memory.sh. Here is the updated code:
#!/bin/bash
# secure_memory.sh - Iron-Clad Protocol for Mac/Linux
# Usage: ./secure_memory.sh lock   (to hide)
# Usage: ./secure_memory.sh unlock (to show)

ACTION=$1
TARGET_DIR=$(pwd)

if [ "$ACTION" == "lock" ]; then
    echo "Locking and hiding environment..."
    # On Mac, use chflags
    if [[ "$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
        chflags hidden .
    fi
    # Restrict permissions to user only
    chmod 700 .
    echo "Protocol Active."
elif [ "$ACTION" == "unlock" ]; then
    echo "Unlocking environment..."
    if [[ "$OSTYPE" == "darwin"* ]]; then
        chflags nohidden .
    fi
    # Restore standard permissions
    chmod 755 .
    echo "Protocol Standby."
else
    echo "Usage: ./secure_memory.sh {lock|unlock}"
fi

toolCalls.name: write_file

args.content:
const {
  Document, Packer, Paragraph, TextRun, Table, TableRow, TableCell,
  AlignmentType, BorderStyle, WidthType, ShadingType, VerticalAlign,
  PageBreak, LevelFormat, HeadingLevel
} = require('docx');
const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const W = 9360;
const cb = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 1, color: "CCCCCC" };
const B  = { top: cb, bottom: cb, left: cb, right: cb };
const tb = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 6, color: "1F4E79" };
const TB = { top: tb, bottom: tb, left: tb, right: tb };
const nb = { style: BorderStyle.NONE, size: 0, color: "FFFFFF" };
const NB = { top: nb, bottom: nb, left: nb, right: nb };

const sp  = (b=60,a=60) => ({before:b, after:a});
const r   = (t, o={}) => new TextRun({ text:t, font:"Arial", size:21, ...o });
const cr  = (t)        => new TextRun({ text:t, font:"Courier New", size:19, color:"C00000" });
const pb  = ()         => new Paragraph({ children:[new PageBreak()] });
const gap = ()         => new Paragraph({ children:[r("")], spacing:sp(50,50) });

// ── paragraph helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function p(runs, align=AlignmentType.LEFT, spacing=sp()) {
  const ch = Array.isArray(runs) ? runs : [r(runs)];
  return new Paragraph({ children:ch, alignment:align, spacing });
}
function cp(text, indent=640) {
  return new Paragraph({ children:[cr(text)], spacing:sp(18,18), indent:{left:indent} });
}
function bp(text, lvl=0) {
  return new Paragraph({
    numbering:{reference:"bullets", level:lvl},
    children:[r(text, {size:20})], spacing:sp(30,20)
  });
}

// ── section header ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function secBar(title, sub) {
  return new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
      borders:TB, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
      shading:{fill:"1F4E79", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
      margins:{top:140, bottom:140, left:240, right:240},
      children:[
        p([r(title,{bold:true, size:30, color:"FFFFFF"})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,30)),
        p([r(sub,  {size:19,  color:"BDD7EE", italics:true})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,0)),
      ]
    })]})],
  });
}

// ── topic card ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function card(icon, title, fromWhat, lines, fill="EAF3FB", codeLines=[]) {
  const content = [];
  // badge row
  content.push(new Table({
    width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W-60],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
      borders:NB, width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA},
      shading:{fill:"2E75B6", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
      margins:{top:60, bottom:60, left:160, right:160},
      children:[p([
        r(icon+" ", {size:22, color:"FFFFFF"}),
        r(title,    {bold:true, size:22, color:"FFFFFF"}),
        r("   [From: "+fromWhat+"]", {size:18, color:"BDD7EE", italics:true}),
      ], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,0))]
    })]})],
  }));
  // explanation lines
  lines.forEach(l => {
    content.push(new Paragraph({ children:[r(l, {size:20})], spacing:sp(28,20), indent:{left:160} }));
  });
  // code block if any
  if (codeLines.length > 0) {
    content.push(new Table({
      width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W-60],
      rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"F5F5F5", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:200, right:200},
        children:codeLines.map(l=>new Paragraph({children:[cr(l)], spacing:sp(16,16)}))
      })]})],
    }));
  }
  return new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
      borders:TB, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
      shading:{fill, type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
      margins:{top:0, bottom:100, left:0, right:0},
      children:content
    })]})],
  });
}

// ── two-col QA block ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function qaBlock(q, aLines) {
  return new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[Math.floor(W*0.42), Math.floor(W*0.58)],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[
      new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:Math.floor(W*0.42), type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"FFF9E6", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:160, right:160},
        children:[
          p([r("Q: ", {bold:true, color:"C00000", size:20}), r(q, {size:20})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,0))
        ]
      }),
      new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:Math.floor(W*0.58), type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"F0F7EE", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:160, right:160},
        children: aLines.map(l => {
          if (l.startsWith("CODE|")) return new Paragraph({children:[cr(l.slice(5))], spacing:sp(16,16)});
          return p([r(l, {size:19})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(20,16));
        })
      }),
    ]})]
  });
}

// ── full-width code box ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function codeBox(title, lines) {
  return new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
    rows:[
      new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"2E75B6", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:50, bottom:50, left:160, right:160},
        children:[p([r(title, {bold:true, size:19, color:"FFFFFF"})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,0))]
      })]}),
      new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
        borders:B, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
        shading:{fill:"F8F8F8", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:200, right:200},
        children:lines.map(l=>new Paragraph({children:[cr(l)], spacing:sp(15,15)}))
      })]})
    ]
  });
}

// ── h2/h3 helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
function h2(text) {
  return new Paragraph({
    children:[r(text, {bold:true, size:25, color:"1F4E79"})],
    spacing:sp(200,80),
    border:{bottom:{style:BorderStyle.SINGLE, size:4, color:"2E75B6", space:4}}
  });
}
function h3(text) {
  return new Paragraph({
    children:[r(text, {bold:true, size:22, color:"375623"})],
    spacing:sp(160,60)
  });
}

// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
// CONTENT
// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
const children = [

  // ── TITLE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
      borders:TB, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
      shading:{fill:"1F4E79", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
      margins:{top:160, bottom:160, left:300, right:300},
      children:[
        p([r("R PROGRAMMING — REMAINING IMPORTANT TOPICS",{bold:true,size:32,color:"FFFFFF"})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,30)),
        p([r("Everything left from PYQ • CA1 • CA4 • PCA2 • Home Assignment • CA3 Topics",{size:20,color:"BDD7EE",italics:true})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,20)),
        p([r("BSc Biotechnology / Bioinformatics / AI  |  Semester 6  |  MAKAUT",{size:19,color:"9DC3E6"})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,0)),
      ]
    })]})],
  }),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 1. FILE I/O ══════════════
  secBar("1.  File Input / Output in R", "From: End Semester PYQ Q6A/Q6B  +  CA4 Q10"),
  gap(),
  h2("1.1  Reading Data"),
  codeBox("Read CSV file", [
    "# Method 1: direct path",
    "df <- read.csv('data.csv')",
    "",
    "# Method 2: GUI file picker",
    "df <- read.csv(file.choose())",
    "",
    "# Method 3: from URL",
    "df <- read.csv(url('http://example.com/data.csv'))",
    "",
    "# Inspect after reading",
    "head(df)       # first 6 rows",
    "tail(df)       # last 6 rows",
    "str(df)        # structure (column types)",
    "nrow(df)       # number of rows",
    "ncol(df)       # number of columns",
    "summary(df)    # basic statistics",
  ]),
  gap(),
  codeBox("Read Excel file (readxl package)", [
    "library(readxl)",
    "df <- read_excel('enzyme_data.xlsx', sheet = 1)",
    "head(df)",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("1.2  Writing Data"),
  codeBox("Write CSV and Excel", [
    "# Write CSV",
    "write.csv(df, 'results.csv', row.names = FALSE)",
    "",
    "# Write Excel (openxlsx package)",
    "library(openxlsx)",
    "write.xlsx(df, 'enzyme_results.xlsx')",
    "",
    "# Write output to a text file using sink()",
    "sink('output.txt')",
    "cat('Results:\\n')",
    "print(df)",
    "cat('Average:', mean(df$Salary), '\\n')",
    "sink()   # IMPORTANT: always close sink()",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("1.3  Full Enzyme CSV Analysis Program (from PYQ Q6A)"),
  codeBox("Enzyme dataset analysis — max, filter, save", [
    "# Assume input.csv has: Enzyme_ID, Enzyme_Name, Category, Activity_Level",
    "df <- read.csv('input.csv')",
    "",
    "# (i) Maximum activity level",
    "max_activity <- max(df$Activity_Level)",
    "cat('Max Activity:', max_activity, '\\n')",
    "",
    "# (ii) Record of enzyme with highest activity",
    "top_enzyme <- df[df$Activity_Level == max_activity, ]",
    "print(top_enzyme)",
    "",
    "# (iii) Filter by category AND activity above threshold",
    "category_filter <- 'Oxidoreductase'",
    "threshold       <- 80",
    "filtered <- df[df$Category == category_filter &",
    "               df$Activity_Level > threshold, ]",
    "print(filtered)",
    "",
    "# (iv) Save all results to result.csv",
    "write.csv(filtered, 'result.csv', row.names = FALSE)",
    "cat('Saved to result.csv\\n')",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 2. STRING OPERATIONS ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("2.  String Operations in R", "From: PYQ Q7B  +  Home Assignment (DNA/Protein functions)"),
  gap(),
  h2("2.1  Key String Functions"),
  new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[2200, 3400, 3760],
    rows:[
      new TableRow({children:[
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:"1F4E79",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:2200,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:60,bottom:60,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r("Function",{bold:true,color:"FFFFFF",size:20})],AlignmentType.CENTER,sp(0,0))]}),
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:"1F4E79",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3400,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:60,bottom:60,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r("What it does",{bold:true,color:"FFFFFF",size:20})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:"1F4E79",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3760,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:60,bottom:60,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r("Example",{bold:true,color:"FFFFFF",size:20})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
      ]}),
      ...([
        ["nchar(x)", "Length of string", "nchar('ATGC')  # 4"],
        ["toupper(x)", "Convert to uppercase", "toupper('atgc')  # 'ATGC'"],
        ["tolower(x)", "Convert to lowercase", "tolower('ATGC')  # 'atgc'"],
        ["substr(x,s,e)", "Extract substring", "substr('ATGCAT',1,3)  # 'ATG'"],
        ["strsplit(x,s)", "Split string into list", "strsplit('ATG','')[[1]]  # 'A' 'T' 'G'"],
        ["paste(x,y)", "Concatenate strings", "paste('Gene','A')  # 'Gene A'"],
        ["paste0(x,y)", "Concatenate no space", "paste0('Gene','A')  # 'GeneA'"],
        ["gsub(p,r,x)", "Replace all matches", "gsub('[^GC]','','ATGC')  # 'GC'"],
        ["grep(p,x)", "Find matching indices", "grep('TP',c('TP53','MYC'))  # 1"],
        ["grepl(p,x)", "Returns TRUE/FALSE", "grepl('ATG','CATGATG')  # TRUE"],
        ["chartr(o,n,x)", "Character-by-char replace", "chartr('AT','TA','ATGC')  # 'TAGC'"],
        ["nchar(gsub(...))", "Count specific chars", "nchar(gsub('[^G]','','ATGCG'))  # 2"],
      ]).map(([fn,desc,ex],i) => new TableRow({children:[
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"DEEAF1":"FFFFFF",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:2200,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:50,bottom:50,left:120,right:120}, children:[new Paragraph({children:[cr(fn)], spacing:sp(0,0)})]}),
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"DEEAF1":"FFFFFF",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3400,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:50,bottom:50,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r(desc,{size:19})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"DEEAF1":"FFFFFF",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3760,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:50,bottom:50,left:120,right:120}, children:[new Paragraph({children:[cr(ex)], spacing:sp(0,0)})]}),
      ]}))
    ]
  }),
  gap(),
  h2("2.2  ATG Codon Finder (from PYQ Q7A iii)"),
  codeBox("Find all positions of a given codon in a DNA sequence", [
    "find_codon <- function(dna, codon = 'ATG') {",
    "  dna      <- toupper(dna)",
    "  codon    <- toupper(codon)",
    "  codon_len <- nchar(codon)",
    "  positions <- c()",
    "",
    "  for (i in 1:(nchar(dna) - codon_len + 1)) {",
    "    if (substr(dna, i, i + codon_len - 1) == codon) {",
    "      positions <- c(positions, i)",
    "    }",
    "  }",
    "",
    "  if (length(positions) == 0) {",
    "    cat(codon, 'not found in sequence\\n')",
    "  } else {",
    "    cat('Codon', codon, 'found at positions:', positions, '\\n')",
    "  }",
    "  return(positions)",
    "}",
    "",
    "find_codon('ATGATCATGATCGGCATG')  # Positions: 1 7 16",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 3. APPLY FAMILY ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("3.  apply() Family Functions", "From: PYQ Q7A  +  Home Assignment  +  End Semester PYQ Q1.vii"),
  gap(),
  h2("3.1  apply() — for matrices"),
  codeBox("apply() on matrix rows and columns", [
    "m <- matrix(c(5.2,8.5,7.1,3.1,6.2,5.8,7.8,4.1,9.2), nrow=3, byrow=TRUE)",
    "rownames(m) <- c('TP53','BRCA1','EGFR')",
    "colnames(m) <- c('Control','Treat1','Treat2')",
    "",
    "apply(m, 1, mean)   # MARGIN=1 -> row means (mean per gene)",
    "apply(m, 2, mean)   # MARGIN=2 -> col means (mean per condition)",
    "apply(m, 1, sum)    # row sums",
    "apply(m, 2, max)    # column maximums",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("3.2  lapply() and sapply() — for lists"),
  codeBox("lapply returns list; sapply returns simplified vector", [
    "expr_list <- list(TP53=c(5.2,8.5,7.1), BRCA1=c(3.1,6.2,5.8), EGFR=c(7.8,4.1,9.2))",
    "",
    "lapply(expr_list, mean)   # returns a LIST of means",
    "sapply(expr_list, mean)   # returns a NAMED VECTOR — simpler",
    "# TP53  BRCA1   EGFR",
    "# 6.93   5.03   7.03",
    "",
    "sapply(expr_list, length) # length of each element",
    "sapply(expr_list, max)    # max of each element",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("3.3  tapply() and aggregate() — for groups (from PYQ Q1.vii)"),
  codeBox("Group-wise statistics on data frames", [
    "df <- data.frame(",
    "  group = c('Control','Control','Treated','Treated','Treated'),",
    "  value = c(5.2, 6.1, 8.5, 9.2, 7.8))",
    "",
    "# tapply: mean of 'value' for each unique 'group'",
    "tapply(df$value, df$group, mean)",
    "# Control: 5.65   Treated: 8.50",
    "",
    "# aggregate: same result, data-frame output",
    "aggregate(value ~ group, df, mean)",
    "",
    "# Both A and C correct — important MCQ point!",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 4. NA HANDLING ══════════════
  secBar("4.  Handling Missing Values (NA)", "From: PYQ Q1.viii  +  CA4 MCQ"),
  gap(),
  codeBox("NA handling — commonly tested patterns", [
    "v <- c(5, 2, NA, 8, NA, 4)",
    "",
    "mean(v)                  # NA  (NA propagates by default)",
    "mean(v, na.rm = TRUE)    # 4.75 (NA removed before calc)",
    "sum(v,  na.rm = TRUE)    # 19",
    "var(v,  na.rm = FALSE)   # NA  (MCQ: na.rm=FALSE -> NA)",
    "var(v,  na.rm = TRUE)    # 5.58",
    "",
    "# Check for NA",
    "is.na(v)                 # FALSE FALSE TRUE FALSE TRUE FALSE",
    "",
    "# Replace NA with 0",
    "v[is.na(v)] <- 0",
    "",
    "# Correct way in ifelse (WRONG: x==NA; CORRECT: is.na(x))",
    "x <- NA",
    "ifelse(x == NA,  1, 0)   # NA  (WRONG)",
    "ifelse(is.na(x), 1, 0)   # 1   (CORRECT)",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 5. SORTING & SUBSET ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("5.  Sorting, Filtering & Subset of Data Frames", "From: End Semester PYQ (Lab Exp 7)  +  CA4 Q10"),
  gap(),
  h2("5.1  Sorting with order()"),
  codeBox("Sort data frame rows by column values", [
    "emp <- data.frame(",
    "  Name   = c('Alice','Bob','Carol','Dave','Eve'),",
    "  Dept   = c('IT','HR','IT','Finance','IT'),",
    "  Salary = c(60000, 45000, 72000, 55000, 68000))",
    "",
    "# Sort ascending by Salary",
    "emp[order(emp$Salary), ]",
    "",
    "# Sort descending by Salary",
    "emp[order(-emp$Salary), ]",
    "emp[order(emp$Salary, decreasing = TRUE), ]",
    "",
    "# Sort by Department first, then by Salary",
    "emp[order(emp$Dept, emp$Salary), ]",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("5.2  subset() function"),
  codeBox("subset() — cleaner filtering syntax", [
    "# Employees with Salary > 60000",
    "subset(emp, Salary > 60000)",
    "",
    "# IT department only",
    "subset(emp, Dept == 'IT')",
    "",
    "# IT department with Salary > 60000 — select only Name & Salary",
    "subset(emp, Dept == 'IT' & Salary > 60000, select = c(Name, Salary))",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("5.3  Full Employee Data Frame Program (from CA4 Q10)"),
  codeBox("Complete employee program with file output", [
    "# a) Create data frame",
    "emp <- data.frame(",
    "  EmployeeID = c(101,102,103,104),",
    "  Name       = c('Alice','Bob','Carol','Dave'),",
    "  Department = c('IT','HR','IT','Finance'),",
    "  Salary     = c(60000,45000,72000,55000),",
    "  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)",
    "",
    "# b) Add new employee + update salary",
    "new_row <- data.frame(EmployeeID=105, Name='Eve', Department='IT', Salary=68000)",
    "emp     <- rbind(emp, new_row)",
    "emp$Salary[emp$Name == 'Bob'] <- 50000   # update Bob's salary",
    "",
    "# c) Filter IT department",
    "it <- emp[emp$Department == 'IT', ]",
    "",
    "# d) Average salary in IT",
    "avg <- mean(it$Salary)",
    "",
    "# e) Write all results to output.txt",
    "sink('output.txt')",
    "cat('=== All Employees ===\\n');   print(emp)",
    "cat('\\n=== IT Department ===\\n'); print(it)",
    "cat('\\nAverage IT Salary: Rs.', avg, '\\n')",
    "sink()   # close the connection",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 7. DPLYR & TIDYVERSE ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("7.  dplyr & tidyverse Packages", "From: CA3 Presentation Topics  +  End Semester PYQ Q4A"),
  gap(),
  h2("7.1  Core dplyr Verbs"),
  codeBox("dplyr — the grammar of data manipulation", [
    "library(dplyr)",
    "",
    "gene_df <- data.frame(",
    "  gene  = c('TP53','BRCA1','EGFR','MYC','PTEN'),",
    "  logFC = c(2.1, -1.5, 0.8, 3.2, -0.3),",
    "  pval  = c(0.001,0.005,0.08,0.0001,0.5))",
    "",
    "# filter() — keep rows matching condition",
    "gene_df %>% filter(logFC > 1)",
    "",
    "# arrange() — sort rows",
    "gene_df %>% arrange(desc(logFC))",
    "",
    "# select() — pick columns",
    "gene_df %>% select(gene, logFC)",
    "",
    "# mutate() — add/modify columns",
    "gene_df %>% mutate(neg_log_p = -log10(pval))",
    "",
    "# Chaining multiple steps with pipe %>%",
    "gene_df %>%",
    "  filter(logFC > 1) %>%",
    "  arrange(desc(logFC)) %>%",
    "  select(gene, logFC, pval)",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("7.2  tidyr — reshape data"),
  codeBox("pivot_longer and pivot_wider", [
    "library(tidyr)",
    "",
    "# Wide format (one row per gene)",
    "wide <- data.frame(",
    "  gene    = c('TP53','BRCA1'),",
    "  control = c(5.2, 3.1),",
    "  treated = c(8.5, 6.2))",
    "",
    "# Convert to LONG format (tidy format)",
    "long <- pivot_longer(wide,",
    "                     cols     = c(control, treated),",
    "                     names_to = 'condition',",
    "                     values_to= 'expression')",
    "print(long)",
    "",
    "# Convert back to WIDE format",
    "wide2 <- pivot_wider(long, names_from='condition', values_from='expression')",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("7.3  ggplot2 — Grammar of Graphics"),
  codeBox("ggplot2 layer-by-layer plot building", [
    "library(ggplot2)",
    "",
    "# Scatter plot — iris dataset",
    "ggplot(iris, aes(x=Sepal.Length, y=Petal.Length, color=Species)) +",
    "  geom_point(size=2.5) +",
    "  labs(title='Iris Dataset', x='Sepal Length', y='Petal Length') +",
    "  theme_minimal()",
    "",
    "# Bar chart",
    "df <- data.frame(country=c('Brazil','Canada','Mexico'), value=c(45,70,30))",
    "ggplot(df, aes(x=country, y=value, fill=country)) +",
    "  geom_bar(stat='identity') +",

    "",

        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"DEEAF1":"FFFFFF",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3860,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:50,bottom:50,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r(src,{size:19})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
      ]}))
    ]
  }),
  gap(),
  h2("9.2  Installing Bioconductor Packages"),
  codeBox("Install and use Bioconductor packages", [
    "# Install BiocManager first",
    "install.packages('BiocManager')",
    "",
    "# Then install Bioconductor packages",
    "BiocManager::install('DESeq2')",
    "BiocManager::install('edgeR')",
    "BiocManager::install('limma')",
    "",
    "# Load and use",
    "library(DESeq2)",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("9.3  GEO2R — What It Is (from PYQ Q1.iv)"),
  p([r("GEO2R is a web-based tool from NCBI GEO (Gene Expression Omnibus) that lets researchers compare two or more groups of samples in a GEO series. It uses the limma R package internally and applies Quantile Normalization by default before differential expression analysis.", {size:20})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,40)),
  qaBlock("Which normalization method does GEO2R apply by default?", [
    "Quantile Normalization (Answer C in PYQ).",
    "It ensures all samples have the same",
    "expression distribution before comparison.",
    "NOT RPKM, TPM, or DESeq2.",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 10. PACKAGE MANAGEMENT ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("10.  Package Management & Comments in R", "From: End Semester PYQ Q3A  +  Lab Experiment 1"),
  gap(),
  h2("10.1  Installing & Loading Packages"),
  codeBox("Package commands — likely short answer", [
    "# Install from CRAN",
    "install.packages('ggplot2')",
    "install.packages(c('dplyr','tidyr','stringr'))  # multiple at once",
    "",
    "# Load into current session",
    "library(ggplot2)",
    "require(ggplot2)  # same but returns TRUE/FALSE",
    "",
    "# Get help on package",
    "help(package = 'ggplot2')",
    "vignette(package = 'ggplot2')",
    "",
    "# Install from Bioconductor",
    "if (!require('BiocManager')) install.packages('BiocManager')",
    "BiocManager::install('DESeq2')",
    "",
    "# Check installed packages",
    "installed.packages()[,'Package']",
    "packageVersion('ggplot2')",
  ]),
  gap(),
  h2("10.2  Adding Comments in R Scripts"),
  codeBox("Single-line and multi-line comments", [
    "# This is a single-line comment in R",
    "",
    "x <- 42  # inline comment after code",
    "",
    "# R does NOT have native multi-line comment syntax.",
    "# The workaround is to use multiple # lines:",
    "# Line 1 of comment",
    "# Line 2 of comment",
    "# Line 3 of comment",
    "",
    "# Alternative: use a string (not assigned, so ignored)",
    "' This is sometimes used as a block comment",
    "  but it is NOT official R syntax. '",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 11. CBIND / RBIND ══════════════
  secBar("11.  cbind() and rbind() — Binding Vectors & Matrices", "From: PYQ Q3B / Q4B  +  CA4"),
  gap(),
  codeBox("Row-wise and column-wise binding with output", [
    "v1 <- c(1, 2, 3)",
    "v2 <- c(4, 5, 6)",
    "",
    "# Column binding — creates a 3x2 matrix",
    "cbind(v1, v2)",
    "#      v1  v2",
    "# [1,]  1   4",
    "# [2,]  2   5",
    "# [3,]  3   6",
    "",
    "# Row binding — creates a 2x3 matrix",
    "rbind(v1, v2)",
    "#     [,1] [,2] [,3]",
    "# v1    1    2    3",
    "# v2    4    5    6",
    "",
    "# Add a new column to existing matrix",
    "m <- matrix(1:9, nrow=3)",
    "new_col <- c(10, 11, 12)",
    "m2 <- cbind(m, new_col)   # now 3x4",
    "",
    "# Add a new row to existing matrix",
    "m3 <- rbind(m, new_row)   # now 4x3",
  ]),
  gap(), gap(),

  // ══════════════ 12. QUICK REFERENCE ══════════════
  pb(),
  secBar("12.  Quick-Reference Cheat Sheet", "Most likely to be asked in MCQ or fill-in-the-blank"),
  gap(),
  new Table({
    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[3000, 3200, 3160],
    rows:[
      new TableRow({children:[
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:"C00000",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3000,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:60,bottom:60,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r("Task",{bold:true,color:"FFFFFF",size:20})],AlignmentType.CENTER,sp(0,0))]}),
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:"C00000",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3200,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:60,bottom:60,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r("R Code",{bold:true,color:"FFFFFF",size:20})],AlignmentType.CENTER,sp(0,0))]}),
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:"C00000",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3160,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:60,bottom:60,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r("Output / Note",{bold:true,color:"FFFFFF",size:20})],AlignmentType.CENTER,sp(0,0))]}),
      ]}),
      ...([
        ["Dimension of matrix","dim(matrix(1:6,nrow=2))","2 3"],
        ["Total elements in matrix","length(matrix(1:9,nrow=3))","9"],
        ["Structure of data frame","str(df)","Shows column types"],
        ["Names of list elements","names(my_list)","Character vector"],
        ["Third element of list","my_list[[3]]","Double brackets"],
        ["Count TRUE values","sum(c(T,F,T,T))","3"],
        ["Sort vector ascending","sort(c(5,2,8,1))","1 2 5 8"],
        ["Reverse a vector","rev(c(1,2,3))","3 2 1"],
        ["Sequence with step","seq(2,10,by=2)","2 4 6 8 10"],
        ["Repeated values","rep(5,3)","5 5 5"],
        ["Which elements TRUE","which(c(T,F,T))","1 3"],
        ["Unique values","unique(c(1,2,1,3))","1 2 3"],
        ["Table of frequencies","table(factor_var)","Counts per level"],
        ["Correlation","cor(x,y,method='pearson')","-1 to 1"],
        ["Remove NAs from mean","mean(v, na.rm=TRUE)","Ignores NA"],
        ["Modulus","7 %% 3","1"],
        ["Integer division","7 %/% 3","2"],
        ["Matrix multiply","A %*% B","Dot product"],
        ["Transpose matrix","t(m)","Rows become cols"],
        ["Column means of matrix","colMeans(m)","Mean of each col"],
        ["Row means of matrix","rowMeans(m)","Mean of each row"],
        ["Add row to data frame","rbind(df, new_row)","New df with extra row"],
        ["Add column to df","df$newcol <- values","In-place addition"],
        ["Delete column from df","df$col <- NULL","Column removed"],
        ["First 6 rows","head(df)","Preview data"],
        ["Last 6 rows","tail(df)","Preview data"],
        ["Number of rows","nrow(df)","Integer"],
        ["Number of cols","ncol(df)","Integer"],
      ]).map(([task,code,note],i)=>new TableRow({children:[
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"FFF2CC":"FFFBEA",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3000,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:45,bottom:45,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r(task,{size:19})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"FFF2CC":"FFFBEA",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3200,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:45,bottom:45,left:120,right:120}, children:[new Paragraph({children:[cr(code)],spacing:sp(0,0)})]}),
        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"FFF2CC":"FFFBEA",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3160,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:45,bottom:45,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r(note,{size:19})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
      ]}))
    ]
  }),
  gap(), gap(),

  // footer
  new Paragraph({
    alignment:AlignmentType.CENTER, spacing:sp(200,40),
    border:{top:{style:BorderStyle.SINGLE, size:4, color:"1F4E79", space:8}},
    children:[r("--- End of Remaining Important Topics ---", {bold:true, size:22, color:"1F4E79"})]
  }),
  new Paragraph({
    alignment:AlignmentType.CENTER, spacing:sp(0,0),
    children:[r("Covers: File I/O  •  Strings & DNA Functions  •  apply()  •  NA Handling  •  All Plot Types  •  dplyr/tidyverse  •  R Markdown  •  GEO2R  •  Packages  •  Cheat Sheet", {size:18, italics:true, color:"595959"})]
  }),
];

// ── build ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
const doc = new Document({
  numbering:{config:[
    {reference:"bullets", levels:[{level:0, format:LevelFormat.BULLET, text:"•", alignment:AlignmentType.LEFT, style:{paragraph:{indent:{left:720,hanging:360}}}}]},
  ]},
  styles:{
    default:{document:{run:{font:"Arial", size:21}}},
    paragraphStyles:[]
  },
  sections:[{
    properties:{page:{size:{width:12240,height:15840}, margin:{top:1080,right:1080,bottom:1080,left:1080}}},
    children
  }]
});

Packer.toBuffer(doc).then(buf=>{
  const outPath = path.join(__dirname, 'Remaining_Important_Topics_R.docx');
  fs.writeFileSync(outPath, buf);
  console.log('File created successfully at:', outPath);
}).catch(console.error);

Index: make_r_doc.js
===================================================================
--- make_r_doc.js	Original
+++ make_r_doc.js	Written
@@ -0,0 +1,895 @@
+const {
+  Document, Packer, Paragraph, TextRun, Table, TableRow, TableCell,
+  AlignmentType, BorderStyle, WidthType, ShadingType, VerticalAlign,
+  PageBreak, LevelFormat, HeadingLevel
+} = require('docx');
+const fs = require('fs');
+const path = require('path');
+
+const W = 9360;
+const cb = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 1, color: "CCCCCC" };
+const B  = { top: cb, bottom: cb, left: cb, right: cb };
+const tb = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 6, color: "1F4E79" };
+const TB = { top: tb, bottom: tb, left: tb, right: tb };
+const nb = { style: BorderStyle.NONE, size: 0, color: "FFFFFF" };
+const NB = { top: nb, bottom: nb, left: nb, right: nb };
+
+const sp  = (b=60,a=60) => ({before:b, after:a});
+const r   = (t, o={}) => new TextRun({ text:t, font:"Arial", size:21, ...o });
+const cr  = (t)        => new TextRun({ text:t, font:"Courier New", size:19, color:"C00000" });
+const pb  = ()         => new Paragraph({ children:[new PageBreak()] });
+const gap = ()         => new Paragraph({ children:[r("")], spacing:sp(50,50) });
+
+// ── paragraph helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+function p(runs, align=AlignmentType.LEFT, spacing=sp()) {
+  const ch = Array.isArray(runs) ? runs : [r(runs)];
+  return new Paragraph({ children:ch, alignment:align, spacing });
+}
+function cp(text, indent=640) {
+  return new Paragraph({ children:[cr(text)], spacing:sp(18,18), indent:{left:indent} });
+}
+function bp(text, lvl=0) {
+  return new Paragraph({
+    numbering:{reference:"bullets", level:lvl},
+    children:[r(text, {size:20})], spacing:sp(30,20)
+  });
+}
+
+// ── section header ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+function secBar(title, sub) {
+  return new Table({
+    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
+    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
+      borders:TB, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
+      shading:{fill:"1F4E79", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
+      margins:{top:140, bottom:140, left:240, right:240},
+      children:[
+        p([r(title,{bold:true, size:30, color:"FFFFFF"})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,30)),
+        p([r(sub,  {size:19,  color:"BDD7EE", italics:true})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,0)),
+      ]
+    })]})],
+  });
+}
+
+// ── topic card ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+function card(icon, title, fromWhat, lines, fill="EAF3FB", codeLines=[]) {
+  const content = [];
+  // badge row
+  content.push(new Table({
+    width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W-60],
+    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
+      borders:NB, width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA},
+      shading:{fill:"2E75B6", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
+      margins:{top:60, bottom:60, left:160, right:160},
+      children:[p([
+        r(icon+" ", {size:22, color:"FFFFFF"}),
+        r(title,    {bold:true, size:22, color:"FFFFFF"}),
+        r("   [From: "+fromWhat+"]", {size:18, color:"BDD7EE", italics:true}),
+      ], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,0))]
+    })]})],
+  }));
+  // explanation lines
+  lines.forEach(l => {
+    content.push(new Paragraph({ children:[r(l, {size:20})], spacing:sp(28,20), indent:{left:160} }));
+  });
+  // code block if any
+  if (codeLines.length > 0) {
+    content.push(new Table({
+      width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W-60],
+      rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
+        borders:B, width:{size:W-60, type:WidthType.DXA},
+        shading:{fill:"F5F5F5", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
+        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:200, right:200},
+        children:codeLines.map(l=>new Paragraph({children:[cr(l)], spacing:sp(16,16)}))
+      })]})],
+    }));
+  }
+  return new Table({
+    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
+    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
+      borders:TB, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
+      shading:{fill, type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
+      margins:{top:0, bottom:100, left:0, right:0},
+      children:content
+    })]})],
+  });
+}
+
+// ── two-col QA block ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+function qaBlock(q, aLines) {
+  return new Table({
+    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[Math.floor(W*0.42), Math.floor(W*0.58)],
+    rows:[new TableRow({children:[
+      new TableCell({
+        borders:B, width:{size:Math.floor(W*0.42), type:WidthType.DXA},
+        shading:{fill:"FFF9E6", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
+        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:160, right:160},
+        children:[
+          p([r("Q: ", {bold:true, color:"C00000", size:20}), r(q, {size:20})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,0))
+        ]
+      }),
+      new TableCell({
+        borders:B, width:{size:Math.floor(W*0.58), type:WidthType.DXA},
+        shading:{fill:"F0F7EE", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
+        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:160, right:160},
+        children: aLines.map(l => {
+          if (l.startsWith("CODE|")) return new Paragraph({children:[cr(l.slice(5))], spacing:sp(16,16)});
+          return p([r(l, {size:19})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(20,16));
+        })
+      }),
+    ]})]
+  });
+}
+
+// ── full-width code box ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+function codeBox(title, lines) {
+  return new Table({
+    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
+    rows:[
+      new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
+        borders:B, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
+        shading:{fill:"2E75B6", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
+        margins:{top:50, bottom:50, left:160, right:160},
+        children:[p([r(title, {bold:true, size:19, color:"FFFFFF"})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,0))]
+      })]}),
+      new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
+        borders:B, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
+        shading:{fill:"F8F8F8", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
+        margins:{top:80, bottom:80, left:200, right:200},
+        children:lines.map(l=>new Paragraph({children:[cr(l)], spacing:sp(15,15)}))
+      })]})
+    ]
+  });
+}
+
+// ── h2/h3 helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+function h2(text) {
+  return new Paragraph({
+    children:[r(text, {bold:true, size:25, color:"1F4E79"})],
+    spacing:sp(200,80),
+    border:{bottom:{style:BorderStyle.SINGLE, size:4, color:"2E75B6", space:4}}
+  });
+}
+function h3(text) {
+  return new Paragraph({
+    children:[r(text, {bold:true, size:22, color:"375623"})],
+    spacing:sp(160,60)
+  });
+}
+
+// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+// CONTENT
+// ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
+const children = [
+
+  // ── TITLE ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+  new Table({
+    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[W],
+    rows:[new TableRow({children:[new TableCell({
+      borders:TB, width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA},
+      shading:{fill:"1F4E79", type:ShadingType.CLEAR},
+      margins:{top:160, bottom:160, left:300, right:300},
+      children:[
+        p([r("R PROGRAMMING — REMAINING IMPORTANT TOPICS",{bold:true,size:32,color:"FFFFFF"})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,30)),
+        p([r("Everything left from PYQ • CA1 • CA4 • PCA2 • Home Assignment • CA3 Topics",{size:20,color:"BDD7EE",italics:true})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,20)),
+        p([r("BSc Biotechnology / Bioinformatics / AI  |  Semester 6  |  MAKAUT",{size:19,color:"9DC3E6"})], AlignmentType.CENTER, sp(0,0)),
+      ]
+    })]})],
+  }),
+  gap(), gap(),
+
+  // ══════════════ 1. FILE I/O ══════════════
+  secBar("1.  File Input / Output in R", "From: End Semester PYQ Q6A/Q6B  +  CA4 Q10"),
+  gap(),
+  h2("1.1  Reading Data"),
+  codeBox("Read CSV file", [
+    "# Method 1: direct path",
+    "df <- read.csv('data.csv')",
+    "",
+    "# Method 2: GUI file picker",
+    "df <- read.csv(file.choose())",
+    "",
+    "# Method 3: from URL",
+    "df <- read.csv(url('http://example.com/data.csv'))",
+    "",
+    "# Inspect after reading",
+    "head(df)       # first 6 rows",
+    "tail(df)       # last 6 rows",
+    "str(df)        # structure (column types)",
+    "nrow(df)       # number of rows",
+    "ncol(df)       # number of columns",
+    "summary(df)    # basic statistics",
+  ]),
+  gap(),
+  codeBox("Read Excel file (readxl package)", [
+    "library(readxl)",
+    "df <- read_excel('enzyme_data.xlsx', sheet = 1)",
+    "head(df)",
+  ]),
+  gap(),
+  h2("1.2  Writing Data"),
+  codeBox("Write CSV and Excel", [
+    "# Write CSV",
+    "write.csv(df, 'results.csv', row.names = FALSE)",
+    "",
+    "# Write Excel (openxlsx package)",
+    "library(openxlsx)",
+    "write.xlsx(df, 'enzyme_results.xlsx')",
+    "",
+    "# Write output to a text file using sink()",
+    "sink('output.txt')",
+    "cat('Results:\\n')",
+    "print(df)",
+    "cat('Average:', mean(df$Salary), '\\n')",
+    "sink()   # IMPORTANT: always close sink()",
+  ]),
+  gap(),
+  h2("1.3  Full Enzyme CSV Analysis Program (from PYQ Q6A)"),
+  codeBox("Enzyme dataset analysis — max, filter, save", [
+    "# Assume input.csv has: Enzyme_ID, Enzyme_Name, Category, Activity_Level",
+    "df <- read.csv('input.csv')",
+    "",
+    "# (i) Maximum activity level",
+    "max_activity <- max(df$Activity_Level)",
+    "cat('Max Activity:', max_activity, '\\n')",
+    "",
+    "# (ii) Record of enzyme with highest activity",
+    "top_enzyme <- df[df$Activity_Level == max_activity, ]",
+    "print(top_enzyme)",
+    "",
+    "# (iii) Filter by category AND activity above threshold",
+    "category_filter <- 'Oxidoreductase'",
+    "threshold       <- 80",
+    "filtered <- df[df$Category == category_filter &",
+    "               df$Activity_Level > threshold, ]",
+    "print(filtered)",
+    "",
+    "# (iv) Save all results to result.csv",
+    "write.csv(filtered, 'result.csv', row.names = FALSE)",
+    "cat('Saved to result.csv\\n')",
+  ]),
+  gap(), gap(),
+
+  // ══════════════ 2. STRING OPERATIONS ══════════════
+  pb(),
+  secBar("2.  String Operations in R", "From: PYQ Q7B  +  Home Assignment (DNA/Protein functions)"),
+  gap(),
+  h2("2.1  Key String Functions"),
+  new Table({
+    width:{size:W, type:WidthType.DXA}, columnWidths:[2200, 3400, 3760],
+    rows:[
+      new TableRow({children:[
+        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:"1F4E79",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:2200,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:60,bottom:60,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r("Function",{bold:true,color:"FFFFFF",size:20})],AlignmentType.CENTER,sp(0,0))]}),
+        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:"1F4E79",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3400,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:60,bottom:60,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r("What it does",{bold:true,color:"FFFFFF",size:20})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
+        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:"1F4E79",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3760,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:60,bottom:60,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r("Example",{bold:true,color:"FFFFFF",size:20})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
+      ]}),
+      ...([
+        ["nchar(x)", "Length of string", "nchar('ATGC')  # 4"],
+        ["toupper(x)", "Convert to uppercase", "toupper('atgc')  # 'ATGC'"],
+        ["tolower(x)", "Convert to lowercase", "tolower('ATGC')  # 'atgc'"],
+        ["substr(x,s,e)", "Extract substring", "substr('ATGCAT',1,3)  # 'ATG'"],
+        ["strsplit(x,s)", "Split string into list", "strsplit('ATG','')[[1]]  # 'A' 'T' 'G'"],
+        ["paste(x,y)", "Concatenate strings", "paste('Gene','A')  # 'Gene A'"],
+        ["paste0(x,y)", "Concatenate no space", "paste0('Gene','A')  # 'GeneA'"],
+        ["gsub(p,r,x)", "Replace all matches", "gsub('[^GC]','','ATGC')  # 'GC'"],
+        ["grep(p,x)", "Find matching indices", "grep('TP',c('TP53','MYC'))  # 1"],
+        ["grepl(p,x)", "Returns TRUE/FALSE", "grepl('ATG','CATGATG')  # TRUE"],
+        ["chartr(o,n,x)", "Character-by-char replace", "chartr('AT','TA','ATGC')  # 'TAGC'"],
+        ["nchar(gsub(...))", "Count specific chars", "nchar(gsub('[^G]','','ATGCG'))  # 2"],
+      ]).map(([fn,desc,ex],i) => new TableRow({children:[
+        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"DEEAF1":"FFFFFF",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:2200,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:50,bottom:50,left:120,right:120}, children:[new Paragraph({children:[cr(fn)], spacing:sp(0,0)})]}),
+        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"DEEAF1":"FFFFFF",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3400,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:50,bottom:50,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r(desc,{size:19})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),

+  h2("2.2  ATG Codon Finder (from PYQ Q7A iii)"),
+  codeBox("Find all positions of a given codon in a DNA sequence", [
+    "find_codon <- function(dna, codon = 'ATG') {",
+    "  dna      <- toupper(dna)",
+    "  codon    <- toupper(codon)",
+    "  codon_len <- nchar(codon)",
+    "  positions <- c()",
+    "",
+    "  for (i in 1:(nchar(dna) - codon_len + 1)) {",
+    "    if (substr(dna, i, i + codon_len - 1) == codon) {",
+    "      positions <- c(positions, i)",
+    "    }",
+    "  }",
+    "",
+    "  if (length(positions) == 0) {",
+    "    cat(codon, 'not found in sequence\\n')",
+    "  } else {",
+    "    cat('Codon', codon, 'found at positions:', positions, '\\n')",
+    "  }",
+    "  return(positions)",
+    "}",
+    "",
+    "find_codon('ATGATCATGATCGGCATG')  # Positions: 1 7 16",
+  ]),
+  gap(), gap(),

+  secBar("3.  apply() Family Functions", "From: PYQ Q7A  +  Home Assignment  +  End Semester PYQ Q1.vii"),

+  h2("3.1  apply() — for matrices"),
+  codeBox("apply() on matrix rows and columns", [
+    "m <- matrix(c(5.2,8.5,7.1,3.1,6.2,5.8,7.8,4.1,9.2), nrow=3, byrow=TRUE)",
+    "rownames(m) <- c('TP53','BRCA1','EGFR')",
+    "colnames(m) <- c('Control','Treat1','Treat2')",
+    "",
+    "apply(m, 1, mean)   # MARGIN=1 -> row means (mean per gene)",
+    "apply(m, 2, mean)   # MARGIN=2 -> col means (mean per condition)",
+    "apply(m, 1, sum)    # row sums",
+    "apply(m, 2, max)    # column maximums",
+  ]),
+  gap(),
+  h2("3.2  lapply() and sapply() — for lists"),
+  codeBox("lapply returns list; sapply returns simplified vector", [
+    "expr_list <- list(TP53=c(5.2,8.5,7.1), BRCA1=c(3.1,6.2,5.8), EGFR=c(7.8,4.1,9.2))",
+    "",
+    "lapply(expr_list, mean)   # returns a LIST of means",
+    "sapply(expr_list, mean)   # returns a NAMED VECTOR — simpler",
+    "# TP53  BRCA1   EGFR",
+    "# 6.93   5.03   7.03",
+    "",
+    "sapply(expr_list, length) # length of each element",
+    "sapply(expr_list, max)    # max of each element",
+  ]),
+  gap(),
+  h2("3.3  tapply() and aggregate() — for groups (from PYQ Q1.vii)"),
+  codeBox("Group-wise statistics on data frames", [
+    "df <- data.frame(",
+    "  group = c('Control','Control','Treated','Treated','Treated'),",
+    "  value = c(5.2, 6.1, 8.5, 9.2, 7.8))",
+    "",
+    "# tapply: mean of 'value' for each unique 'group'",
+    "tapply(df$value, df$group, mean)",
+    "# Control: 5.65   Treated: 8.50",
+    "",
+    "# aggregate: same result, data-frame output",
+    "aggregate(value ~ group, df, mean)",
+    "",
+    "# Both A and C correct — important MCQ point!",
+  ]),

+  secBar("4.  Handling Missing Values (NA)", "From: PYQ Q1.viii  +  CA4 MCQ"),
+  gap(),
+  codeBox("NA handling — commonly tested patterns", [
+    "v <- c(5, 2, NA, 8, NA, 4)",
+    "",
+    "mean(v)                  # NA  (NA propagates by default)",
+    "mean(v, na.rm = TRUE)    # 4.75 (NA removed before calc)",
+    "sum(v,  na.rm = TRUE)    # 19",
+    "var(v,  na.rm = FALSE)   # NA  (MCQ: na.rm=FALSE -> NA)",
+    "var(v,  na.rm = TRUE)    # 5.58",
+    "",
+    "# Check for NA",
+    "is.na(v)                 # FALSE FALSE TRUE FALSE TRUE FALSE",
+    "",
+    "# Replace NA with 0",
+    "v[is.na(v)] <- 0",
+    "",
+    "# Correct way in ifelse (WRONG: x==NA; CORRECT: is.na(x))",
+    "x <- NA",
+    "ifelse(x == NA,  1, 0)   # NA  (WRONG)",
+    "ifelse(is.na(x), 1, 0)   # 1   (CORRECT)",
+  ]),
+  gap(), gap(),

+  pb(),
+  secBar("5.  Sorting, Filtering & Subset of Data Frames", "From: End Semester PYQ (Lab Exp 7)  +  CA4 Q10"),
+  gap(),
+  h2("5.1  Sorting with order()"),
+  codeBox("Sort data frame rows by column values", [
+    "emp <- data.frame(",
+    "  Name   = c('Alice','Bob','Carol','Dave','Eve'),",
+    "  Dept   = c('IT','HR','IT','Finance','IT'),",
+    "  Salary = c(60000, 45000, 72000, 55000, 68000))",
+    "",
+    "# Sort ascending by Salary",
+    "emp[order(emp$Salary), ]",
+    "",
+    "# Sort descending by Salary",
+    "emp[order(-emp$Salary), ]",
+    "emp[order(emp$Salary, decreasing = TRUE), ]",
+    "",
+    "# Sort by Department first, then by Salary",
+    "emp[order(emp$Dept, emp$Salary), ]",
+  ]),
+  gap(),
+  h2("5.2  subset() function"),
+  codeBox("subset() — cleaner filtering syntax", [
+    "# Employees with Salary > 60000",
+    "subset(emp, Salary > 60000)",
+    "",
+    "# IT department only",
+    "subset(emp, Dept == 'IT')",
+    "",
+    "# IT department with Salary > 60000 — select only Name & Salary",
+    "subset(emp, Dept == 'IT' & Salary > 60000, select = c(Name, Salary))",
+  ]),
+  gap(),
+  h2("5.3  Full Employee Data Frame Program (from CA4 Q10)"),
+  codeBox("Complete employee program with file output", [
+    "# a) Create data frame",
+    "emp <- data.frame(",
+    "  EmployeeID = c(101,102,103,104),",
+    "  Name       = c('Alice','Bob','Carol','Dave'),",
+    "  Department = c('IT','HR','IT','Finance'),",
+    "  Salary     = c(60000,45000,72000,55000),",
+    "  stringsAsFactors = FALSE)",
+    "",
+    "# b) Add new employee + update salary",
+    "new_row <- data.frame(EmployeeID=105, Name='Eve', Department='IT', Salary=68000)",
+    "emp     <- rbind(emp, new_row)",
+    "emp$Salary[emp$Name == 'Bob'] <- 50000   # update Bob's salary",
+    "",
+    "# c) Filter IT department",
+    "it <- emp[emp$Department == 'IT', ]",
+    "",
+    "# d) Average salary in IT",
+    "avg <- mean(it$Salary)",
+    "",
+    "# e) Write all results to output.txt",
+    "sink('output.txt')",
+    "cat('=== All Employees ===\\n');   print(emp)",
+    "cat('\\n=== IT Department ===\\n'); print(it)",
+    "cat('\\nAverage IT Salary: Rs.', avg, '\\n')",
+    "sink()   # close the connection",
+  ]),

+  codeBox("dplyr — the grammar of data manipulation", [
+    "library(dplyr)",
+    "",
+    "gene_df <- data.frame(",
+    "  gene  = c('TP53','BRCA1','EGFR','MYC','PTEN'),",
+    "  logFC = c(2.1, -1.5, 0.8, 3.2, -0.3),",
+    "  pval  = c(0.001,0.005,0.08,0.0001,0.5))",
+    "",
+    "# filter() — keep rows matching condition",
+    "gene_df %>% filter(logFC > 1)",
+    "",
+    "# arrange() — sort rows",
+    "gene_df %>% arrange(desc(logFC))",
+    "",
+    "# select() — pick columns",
+    "gene_df %>% select(gene, logFC)",
+    "",
+    "# mutate() — add/modify columns",
+    "gene_df %>% mutate(neg_log_p = -log10(pval))",
+    "",
+    "# Chaining multiple steps with pipe %>%",
+    "gene_df %>%",
+    "  filter(logFC > 1) %>%",
+    "  arrange(desc(logFC)) %>%",
+    "  select(gene, logFC, pval)",
+  ]),

+        ["openxlsx","Read and write Excel files","CRAN"],
+      ]).map(([pkg,pur,src],i)=>new TableRow({children:[
+        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"DEEAF1":"FFFFFF",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:2000,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:50,bottom:50,left:120,right:120}, children:[new Paragraph({children:[cr(pkg)],spacing:sp(0,0)})]}),
+        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"DEEAF1":"FFFFFF",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3500,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:50,bottom:50,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r(pur,{size:19})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
+        new TableCell({borders:B, shading:{fill:i%2===0?"DEEAF1":"FFFFFF",type:ShadingType.CLEAR}, width:{size:3860,type:WidthType.DXA}, margins:{top:50,bottom:50,left:120,right:120}, children:[p([r(src,{size:19})],AlignmentType.LEFT,sp(0,0))]}),
+      ]}))

+  gap(),

It uses the limma R package internally and applies Quantile Normalization by default before differential expression analysis.", {size:20})], AlignmentType.LEFT, sp(0,40)),

+  pb(),

+    "# Install from Bioconductor",
